title: asclepias hastata
intro: Asclepias hastata, also known as green antelopehorn milkweed, is a species of milkweed native to the prairies of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can aid in wound healing and reduce fever. The health benefits of Asclepias hastata are attributed to its ability to treat various ailments, such as fever, sore throats, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to stimulate the immune system. Herbal preparations of Asclepias hastata can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or oil to create teas, tinctures, and salves that can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
